data.name: HintInlineCompletionMode
data.value: Fast: 128 tokens, debounce 300 ms — Low latency, shorter suggestions. Default: 256 tokens, debounce 600 ms — Balanced. High Accuracy: 512 tokens, debounce 1 s — Best quality, slower response.

data.name: HintInlineCompletionEnabled
data.value: When checked, Inferpal shows AI-generated code suggestions inline as you type (ghost text). Press Tab to accept, Escape to dismiss.

data.name: HintInlineCompletionModel
data.value: Model used for Fill-in-the-Middle completions. Leave empty to reuse the chat model. Set a faster or code-specific model (e.g. qwen2.5-coder) for better inline suggestions.

data.name: LabelCodeActionsModel
data.value: Code actions model (empty = use chat model):

data.name: HintCodeActionsModel
data.value: Model used for Explain, Fix, and Refactor commands. Leave empty to reuse the chat model. Set a larger, more capable model for better code analysis.

data.name: LabelInlineEditModel
data.value: Inline Edit model (empty = use code actions model):

data.name: HintInlineEditModel
data.value: Model used for the Inline Edit feature (Ctrl+Shift+I). Leave empty to fall back to the Code Actions model, then the Chat model. A fast instruction-following model (e.g. qwen2.5-coder) works well here.

data.name: InlineEditDlgHeader
data.value: ✏ Describe the edit (Enter to apply, Esc to cancel)

data.name: InlineEditDlgHint
data.value: e.g. "Add null checks", "Convert to async", "Translate comments to English"

data.name: HintRagAutoContext
data.value: When enabled, each code question silently retrieves the most relevant indexed snippets and adds them to the prompt (skipping anything already attached). Requires background indexing.

data.name: HintRagEnabled
data.value: When enabled, Inferpal indexes all source files in the solution in the background and uses semantic search to provide relevant code context to the AI. Disable to save memory or CPU on large repositories.

data.name: HintRagEmbeddingModel
data.value: Ollama model used to generate embeddings for semantic search. Only dedicated embedding models are listed (nomic-embed-text, mxbai-embed-large, bge-m3, …). Falls back to nomic-embed-text if none is selected.

data.name: HintRagTopK
data.value: Maximum number of code chunks returned by the semantic search tool. Higher values provide more context but increase token usage. Recommended: 3–10.

data.name: HintRagSimilarityThreshold
data.value: Minimum cosine similarity score for a chunk to be included in search results. Chunks below this threshold are discarded as low-quality matches. Recommended: 0.20.

data.name: LabelLspEnabled
data.value: LSP semantic chunking (TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust)

data.name: HintLspEnabled
data.value: When enabled, Inferpal uses installed language servers (typescript-language-server, pylsp/pyright-langserver, gopls, rust-analyzer) to extract precise symbol boundaries from non-C# files during RAG indexing, replacing the sliding-window heuristic with accurate function/class/method chunks. Requires the relevant server to be on PATH.

data.name: HintModelAutoUnload
data.value: When enabled, Inferpal instructs Ollama to unload models from VRAM after a period of inactivity. This frees GPU memory when the AI is idle. The VRAM usage of currently loaded models is shown in the chat header.

data.name: HintModelIdleTimeout
data.value: Minutes of inactivity before Ollama unloads the model from VRAM. Lower values free GPU memory sooner; higher values reduce reload time on the next request. Minimum: 1 minute.

data.name: HintLanguage
data.value: By default Inferpal uses Visual Studio's display language. Select a language here to force a different one. The change takes effect immediately after saving.

data.name: HintProvider
data.value: Local model server to use: Ollama (full hardware-aware features) or an OpenAI-compatible server such as LM Studio, llama.cpp, or vLLM. Takes effect after reloading Visual Studio.

data.name: HintApiKey
data.value: Sent as a Bearer token to OpenAI-compatible servers that require authentication. Leave empty for Ollama and LM Studio (no key needed).

data.name: HintUrl
data.value: Base URL of your local Ollama instance. Default is http://localhost:11434. Change this if Ollama runs on a different host or port.

data.name: HintChatModel
data.value: Default model used for all conversations. This field is required. Use /model <name> to switch models mid-session.

data.name: TooltipSessionPicker
data.value: Select a past session to restore

data.name: TooltipLoadSession
data.value: Load the selected session (or the last one if none)

data.name: DeleteSessionConfirm
data.value:
Delete session "{0}"?
This cannot be undone.

data.name: TooltipStepMode
data.value: Step mode — pause the agent after each tool call (toggle)

data.name: TooltipPlanMode
data.value: Plan mode — read-only: the agent explores and proposes a plan without editing files (toggle)

data.name: PromptExplainSelection
PromptRefactorSelection
PromptAddTestsSelection
data.value:
Write unit tests for the code from `{0}` using the project's existing test framework:
**COVERAGE MAP** — identify all public methods and branches that need testing. **HAPPY PATH** — tests for normal, expected inputs and behavior. **EDGE CASES** — tests for boundary values, empty inputs, and nulls. **FAILURE MODES** — tests for invalid inputs and expected exceptions.
Provide the complete, ready-to-compile test code.
{1}

data.name: PromptFixSelection
PromptAddDocstringSelection
data.value:
Add XML documentation comments to all public types, methods, properties, and parameters in `{0}`:
- <summary> — one sentence: WHAT it does, not HOW. No restating the name. - <param> / <returns> — describe the contract: units, null-safety, valid ranges. - <exception> — document any thrown exceptions and the conditions that trigger them. - <remarks> — only when behavior would surprise a reader: side effects, threading, invariants.
Show the fully documented code.
{1}

data.name: PromptAddDocstringEnhanced
data.value:
Add documentation comments to all public members in `{0}`.
**Documentation rules:** - Summary — one sentence: WHAT it does, not HOW. Never restate the name. - Parameters / return value — describe the contract: units, null-safety, valid ranges. - Exceptions / errors — document what can go wrong and the exact triggering conditions. - Remarks / notes — only for surprising behavior: side effects, threading, invariants. - Inherited and interface-implementation members → defer to the parent doc (`<inheritdoc/>` or `<inheritdoc cref="IFoo.Member"/>`) unless adding extra remarks.
{1}
Output the **complete source file** with all documentation added. Preserve all original code exactly — add documentation only, never refactor.

data.name: PromptReviewSelection
data.value:
Review the code in `{0}` and provide a structured critique:
- **Bugs and edge cases** — logic errors, null dereferences, off-by-one, resource leaks. - **Design and clarity** — naming, single-responsibility, complexity, duplication. - **Performance** — unnecessary allocations, inefficient loops, missing caching. - **Security** — injection risks, improper input validation, secrets in code. - **Tests** — missing coverage, untestable design.
For each issue cite the line or symbol, explain the risk, and suggest a concrete fix.
{1}

data.name: HintCommandTimeout
data.value: Maximum time to wait for a single tool call (e.g. a shell command or build). Does not affect AI response time. Increase if your builds take longer.

data.name: HintToolBubblesExpanded
data.value: When checked, tool call details (inputs and outputs) are shown expanded in the chat. Uncheck to keep them collapsed by default.

data.name: LabelSecurityAlertsDisabled
data.value: Disable security alerts (approve all tool calls silently)

data.name: HintSecurityAlertsDisabled
data.value: When checked, all tool calls (file writes, shell commands, etc.) are approved automatically without any confirmation prompt. Use only in trusted environments.

data.name: HintContextWindowSize
data.value: Match your model's num_ctx setting. Common values: 2048 (small 7B), 4096 (default), 8192, 16384. When the conversation reaches 80% of this limit, the oldest messages are automatically removed and a warning is shown.

data.name: HintContextWindowSizeClientTrim
data.value: On LM Studio / OpenAI-compatible servers the context length is fixed at model load, not per request — this value only drives client-side trimming, so keep it at or below the context the model is loaded with. When the conversation reaches 80% of this limit, the oldest messages are automatically removed and a warning is shown.

data.name: LabelContextWindowKeepTurns
data.value: Turns to always keep when truncating:

data.name: HintContextWindowKeepTurns
data.value: Number of recent user/assistant exchanges to preserve when the context is truncated. The system prompt is always kept regardless. Increase this if the model needs more recent context to stay coherent.

data.name: MsgContextTruncated
data.value: ⚠ Context window: removed {0} old message(s) — keeping last {1} turn(s). Older context is lost.

data.name: LabelCompactionEnabled
data.value: Smart context compaction (summarize instead of truncate)

data.name: HintCompactionEnabled
data.value: When checked, old messages are summarized by the model before being removed, preserving key information. A safety fuse cancels the summary call if it takes too long and falls back to hard truncation.

data.name: HintCompactionTimeout
data.value: Maximum seconds to wait for the compaction model call. If it exceeds this limit, the safety fuse fires and hard truncation is used instead. Default: 45.

data.name: HintKvCacheAnchor
data.value: Number of opening messages (after the system prompt) kept verbatim at the start of every compacted context. Ollama reuses its KV cache for these tokens, saving recalculation time. 0 = disabled. Default: 3.

data.name: MsgContextCompacted
data.value: 📦 Context compacted: {0} message(s) summarized — keeping last {1} turn(s).

data.name: MsgContextCompactionFallback
data.value: ⚠ Context compaction timed out — fell back to hard truncation.

data.name: CompactionSummarizePrompt
data.value:
The following is the beginning of our conversation. Summarize it densely, preserving all key decisions, facts, modified files, and context needed for future messages. Be concise but complete — this summary will replace the original messages:
{0}

data.name: LabelCustomSystemPrompt
data.value: Custom system prompt (appended to the base prompt, before project context):

data.name: HintCustomSystemPrompt
data.value: Additional instructions appended to the built-in system prompt. Use this to set the assistant's tone, restrict its scope, or add domain knowledge specific to your project.

data.name: LabelPinnedContextFiles
data.value: Pinned context files (one path per line, max 3 — always injected into the prompt):

data.name: HintPinnedContextFiles
data.value: Absolute file paths always injected into the system prompt before each request. Use this to keep important architecture docs, interfaces, or schemas in context at all times.

data.name: LabelPromptTemplates
data.value: Custom slash commands (one per line: /name=text, use {args} for extra words):

data.name: HintPromptTemplates
data.value: Define your own slash commands. Format: /mycommand=Explain this in detail: {args}. The {args} placeholder is replaced by any words typed after the command name. Templates appear in autocomplete suggestions.

data.name: HintPermissionRules
data.value: Rules evaluated before the approval prompt. 'allow' auto-approves a matching tool call, 'deny' blocks it, no match falls back to the prompt; first match wins. The regex is matched against the command or file path. Applied on top of the committable .inferpal/permissions.json overlay, plus a built-in non-bypassable denylist of catastrophic commands. Example: allow run_command ^dotnet

data.name: LabelCustomTools
data.value: Custom agent tools (one per line: name=powershell_command):

data.name: HintCustomTools
data.value: Define shell tools the agent can call. Format: my_tool=dotnet test. The tool name must be snake_case. An optional {args} parameter will be appended when the model provides extra arguments. Requires approval before running.

data.name: HintPersonaAutoSwitch
data.value: When checked, the system prompt is automatically enriched with a language-specific hint (C#, Python, TypeScript…) when the active editor document changes.

data.name: LabelOodaTurnThreshold
data.value: OODA recap every N turns (0 = disabled):

data.name: HintOodaTurnThreshold
data.value: Every N conversation turns, the model generates a brief session recap (goal, progress, open questions) injected into the system prompt to prevent context amnesia on long sessions. Set to 0 to disable. Recommended: 10.

data.name: OodaSummarizePrompt
data.value: Summarize our conversation so far. Produce a short OODA-style recap with three labeled sections — Goal (what the user wants to achieve), Progress (steps already completed), and Open (remaining tasks or unresolved questions). Keep the total under 150 words.

data.name: HintSend
data.value: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline · Shift+↑↓ history

data.name: TooltipAttachFile
data.value: Attach active file to next prompt

data.name: TooltipAttachSelection
data.value: Attach selection to next prompt (full file if no selection)

data.name: TooltipBrowseFile
data.value: Browse and attach a file from disk

data.name: TooltipPinFile
data.value: Pin the active file — always kept in context

data.name: PinLimitReached
data.value: ⚠ Pinned files limit reached (max {0}). Unpin one first.

data.name: TooltipSaveSnippet
data.value: Save to snippet library (/snippets to view)

data.name: SystemPrompt
data.value: You are Inferpal, a local AI developer assistant integrated in Visual Studio 2026. You have access to tools to read/write files, list directories, search code, and run PowerShell commands. Use these tools as much as needed before replying. Respond in the same language as the user.

data.name: MsgLoopDetected
data.value: (loop detected — identical tool calls repeated, stopping)

data.name: MsgCircuitOpen
data.value: (circuit breaker open — retries paused for 5 min after repeated failures)

data.name: MsgNoUrl
data.value: Ollama URL not configured. Open Settings to set it.

data.name: MsgEmptyResponse
data.value: The model returned no response. The configured model may not support text generation — try the default chat model.

data.name: MsgTimeout
data.value: Connection timed out — Ollama did not respond at {0} within 30 minutes.

data.name: MsgUnreachable
data.value: Cannot reach Ollama at {0}. Check the URL in Settings.

data.name: MsgServerError
data.value:
The inference server at {0} returned an error:
{1}

data.name: MsgContextOverflow
data.value:
The request is too large for the model's context window in agent mode — the tool definitions alone take several thousand tokens. Increase the model's context length (16384 or more recommended in LM Studio), or reduce the number of enabled agent tools.
Server detail: {0}

data.name: MsgContextWontFit
data.value: The request (~{0} tokens) is larger than the context the model is loaded with ({1} tokens) in agent mode — the tool definitions alone take several thousand tokens. Increase the loaded context length (16384 or more recommended in LM Studio), or reduce the number of enabled agent tools.

data.name: MsgConnectionGuardFailed
DiffOldNotFound
data.value: old_content not found in {0}. Check spaces, line breaks and indentation.

data.name: DiffAmbiguous
data.value: old_content found {0} times in {1}. Provide more context to make the match unique.

data.name: ApplyEditsEmpty
data.value: apply_edits requires at least one edit.

data.name: DiagNoProject
data.value: No .sln or .csproj file found. Provide the path parameter.

data.name: DiagBuildFailed
data.value:
Build failed (code {0}) with no parseable diagnostics.
{1}

data.name: ActiveDocNoContext
data.value: VS context unavailable — open the Inferpal window via the View menu.

data.name: ActiveDocNoFile
data.value: No file open in the editor.

data.name: AttachFileTooLarge
data.value: ⚠ File too large to attach (max 500 KB).

data.name: AttachNoActiveFile
data.value: ⚠ No active file found. Open a file in the editor first.

data.name: MentionDebuggerNone
data.value: ⚠ No paused debug session. Hit a breakpoint or exception first, then use @debugger.

data.name: SlashNoActiveDocument
data.value: ⚠ No active document. Open a file in the editor first.

data.name: TestsExtended
data.value: ✅ Tests added to **{0}** (open — undo with Ctrl+Z).

data.name: TestsGenerateFailed
data.value: ⚠ Test generation failed. Check the model and try again.

data.name: RefactorNoChange
data.value: ✅ The code is already clear — no refactor needed.

data.name: FixNoChange
data.value: ✅ No bug or issue found — nothing to fix.

data.name: DocNoChange
data.value: ✅ The code is already adequately documented.

data.name: TestsNoChange
data.value: ✅ Nothing useful to test — every meaningful case is already covered.

data.name: SlashUsageRestore
data.value: Usage: `/restore <path>` — restores the most recent backup from `.inferpal/history/`

data.name: SlashHelp
data.value:
Unknown command: `{0}`
**Meta** - `/clear` — clear the conversation - `/model <name>` — switch model - `/tools on|off` — enable or disable tool calling - `/export` — export conversation to a file - `/restore <path>` — restore a file from its most recent backup - `/help` — show all available commands
**File system** - `/read <path>` — attach a file as context - `/ls <path> [pattern]` — list files in a directory - `/grep <dir> <pattern> [ext]` — search text in files
**Code actions** - `/explain` — explain the active file (or selection) - `/fix` — suggest fixes for the active file - `/review` — structured code review - `/refactor` — refactor suggestions - `/test` — generate unit tests - `/doc` — generate documentation with namespace/inheritance context (C#/TS/Python/Go/Rust)
**Shell** - `/run <command>` — run a PowerShell command
**Web** - `/fetch <url>` — show a web page as text - `/search <query>` — DuckDuckGo web search
**Git** - `/commit` — analyse the diff, propose a commit message and pre-fill the prompt to confirm - `/git [path]` — show git status, log, and diff summary
**Visual Studio / Build** - `/map` — show project dependency graph - `/solution [path]` — show solution structure - `/build [path]` — compile and show errors - `/fix-build [path]` — compile → AI fixes errors → recompile, repeat until clean (max 5 rounds)
**Project context** - `/context` — show the active `.inferpal/context.md` (injected into every system prompt) - `/memory` — view agent memory (`.inferpal/memory.md`, injected into every system prompt)

data.name: SlashHelpAll
data.value:
**Meta** - `/clear` — clear the conversation - `/model <name>` — switch model - `/tools on|off` — enable or disable tool calling - `/export` — export conversation to a file - `/restore <path>` — restore a file from its most recent backup - `/help` — show all available commands
**File system** - `/read <path>` — attach a file as context - `/ls <path> [pattern]` — list files in a directory - `/grep <dir> <pattern> [ext]` — search text in files
**Code actions** - `/explain` — explain the active file (or selection) - `/fix` — suggest fixes for the active file - `/review` — structured code review - `/refactor` — refactor suggestions - `/test` — generate unit tests - `/doc` — generate documentation with namespace/inheritance context (C#/TS/Python/Go/Rust)
**Shell** - `/run <command>` — run a PowerShell command
**Web** - `/fetch <url>` — show a web page as text - `/search <query>` — DuckDuckGo web search
**Git** - `/commit` — analyse the diff, propose a commit message and pre-fill the prompt to confirm - `/git [path]` — show git status, log, and diff summary - `/diff [path]` — attach full git diff as a context chip
**Visual Studio / Build** - `/map` — show project dependency graph - `/solution [path]` — show solution structure - `/build [path]` — compile and show errors - `/fix-build [path]` — compile → AI fixes errors → recompile, repeat until clean (max 5 rounds)
**Project context** - `/context` — show the active `.inferpal/context.md` (injected into every system prompt) - `/memory` — view agent memory (`.inferpal/memory.md`, injected into every system prompt) - `/note <text>` — append a timestamped note to `.inferpal/notes.md` - `/notes [clear]` — list or clear project notes (injected into every system prompt)
**Semantic index** - `/index` — show RAG index status (chunks, model, root) - `/index rebuild` — force a full re-indexing of the solution
**Snippets & History** - `/snippets` — list saved code snippets (⭐ button on code blocks to save) - `/snippets copy <n>` / `/snippets delete <n>` / `/snippets clear` - `/history` — list all saved sessions with date and first message preview - `/history <term>` — full-text search across all saved sessions - `/phistory [term]` — search prompt input history; `/phistory use <n>` to re-use
**Session templates** - `/template` — list available session presets (code-review, bug-hunt, architecture…) - `/template <id>` — start a new session with the selected template
**Models** - `/models` — list installed Ollama models with VRAM status - `/models pull <name>` — download a model from the registry - `/models delete <name>` — remove a model - `/models running` — show models currently loaded in VRAM - `/hardware` — show the GPU/VRAM profile; `/hardware <gb>` to set the VRAM budget - `/setup` — re-run first-run setup: re-detect backend, re-pick chat and embedding models
**Documentation (@Docs)** - `/docs add <url> [title]` — crawl & index an external doc site for the `search_docs` tool - `/docs list` — list indexed documentation sources - `/docs remove <id>` — remove a documentation source - `/docs reindex [id]` — re-crawl all sources (or one by id)
**Agent** - `/agent-step` — toggle step mode: agent pauses after each tool call - `/resume` — resume agent after a step-mode pause - `/plan` — toggle plan mode: read-only, the agent explores and proposes a plan without editing files
**Rules & Checks** - `/rules` — list project rules (`.inferpal/rules`, glob-scoped, injected into the system prompt) - `/rules init` — create an example rule - `/checks` — list review checks (`.inferpal/checks`) - `/checks init` — create an example check - `/check [name]` — AI-review the current git diff against the checks (100% local)
**Prompt files** - `/prompts` — list reusable prompt files (`.inferpal/prompts/*.md`); each file is a slash command, `{args}` expands - `/prompts init` — create an example prompt file

data.name: HistoryNoSessions
data.value: No saved sessions found. Sessions are archived automatically on `/clear`.

data.name: SlashContextNotFound
data.value:
No project context file found.
Create `{0}` to document your project conventions (coding style, architecture decisions, etc.). It will be injected automatically into every system prompt.
Use `/clear` after creating or editing the file to reload it.

data.name: SlashContextLoaded
data.value:
**Project context** — `{0}` ({1} chars)
``` {2} ```
Use `/clear` after editing the file to reload it.

data.name: GitNotRepo
data.value: Not a git repository (no .git folder found).

data.name: SolutionNoSln
data.value: No .sln file found. Try providing the path explicitly.

data.name: SolutionPathNotFound
data.value: Path not found or not a .sln file: {0}

data.name: FixBuildSuccess
data.value: ✅ Build succeeded after {0} round(s) — no more errors.

data.name: FixBuildGiveUp
data.value: ❌ Build still failing after {0} attempt(s) — manual intervention required.

data.name: BuildFailedProposal
data.value:
❌ **Build failed** — {0} compilation error(s) detected.
Click **Fix with AI** to automatically fix in a loop, or type `/fix-build`.

data.name: SmartFixBuildOk
data.value: ✅ Smart Fix: build OK — no compilation errors.

data.name: SmartFixBuildErrors
data.value: 🔨 Smart Fix: {0} compilation error(s) detected — please fix before continuing: {1}

data.name: SmartFixTimeout
data.value: ⚠ Smart Fix: build check timed out (60 s) — verify manually.

data.name: HintSmartFixEnabled
data.value: When checked, a quick dotnet build is run automatically after each write_file or apply_diff on a C#/project file. Compilation errors are returned inline so the agent can correct them immediately in the same loop.

data.name: CommitNothingStaged
data.value: ℹ Nothing staged — analysing unstaged changes.

data.name: CommitConfirmHint
data.value: Message pre-filled below — edit if needed, then press **Enter** to commit.

data.name: RulesNone
data.value: No project rules. Create `.inferpal/rules/*.md` or run `/rules init`.

data.name: ChecksNone
data.value: No review checks. Create `.inferpal/checks/*.md` or run `/checks init`.

data.name: CheckNoDiff
data.value: Nothing to review — the working tree is clean.

data.name: CheckReviewSystemPrompt
data.value: You are a meticulous, fully local code reviewer. Review the git diff below ONLY against the review checks provided. For each check, state whether it PASSES, or list concrete violations. For every violation give the file and line (file:line), a severity (blocker / warning / nit), and a short, actionable fix. Be concise. Do not invent issues and do not comment on anything outside the checks or the diff. If every check passes, say so clearly.

data.name: CheckUnknownName
data.value: No check named '{0}'. Run `/checks` to list them.

data.name: RulesScaffolded
data.value: Example rule ready at {0}. Edit it — matching rules are injected into the system prompt.

data.name: ChecksScaffolded
data.value: Example check ready at {0}. Edit it, then run `/check` to review your diff.

data.name: PromptsNone
data.value: No prompt files. Create `.inferpal/prompts/*.md` or run `/prompts init`.

data.name: PromptsListHeader
data.value: **Prompt files** (`.inferpal/prompts`) — each file is a slash command, `{args}` expands:

data.name: PromptsScaffolded
data.value: Example prompt ready at {0}. Edit it, then type `/review-security` (file name = command).

data.name: ImpactNoPublicApi
data.value: No public types or exported symbols found in {0}.

data.name: ImpactSymbolNotFound
data.value: Symbol '{0}' not found in the public API of {1}.

data.name: ImpactFooter
data.value: 📊 Blast radius: {0} direct · {1} transitive · {2} tests · {3} entry points

data.name: TraceDepsNoMethods
data.value: No methods or functions detected in {0}.

data.name: TraceDepsFooter
data.value: {0} method(s) · {1} unique callee(s) · {2} resolved across files

data.name: RagIndexNotReady
UpdateMemoryNoProject
data.value: No .sln or .inferpal folder found — cannot locate the memory file.

data.name: UpdateMemoryNoContent
data.value: Content is required for append and replace modes.

data.name: SlashMemoryNotFound
data.value:
No agent memory found.
Ask the agent to use `update_memory` to write its first note, or create the file manually.
Expected path: `{0}`

data.name: SlashMemoryLoaded
data.value:
**Agent memory** — `{0}` ({1} chars)
``` {2} ```
Edit the file or ask the agent to use `update_memory`. Use `/clear` to reload.

data.name: LabelAgentModeEnabled
data.value: Enable autonomous agent mode (Plan → Act → Observe)

data.name: HintAgentModeEnabled
data.value: When enabled, Inferpal generates an explicit step-by-step plan before executing any tools, then runs a structured Plan → Act → Observe loop with live step tracking. Best for complex multi-step tasks. Disable for simple, fast queries.

data.name: LabelAgentModel
data.value: Agent / tools model (empty = use chat model):

data.name: HintAgentModel
data.value: Model used whenever tools are enabled — the agent (Plan → Act → Observe) loop and the basic tool-calling loop. Leave empty to reuse the chat model. A smaller, non-multimodal model with prompt-cache reuse (e.g. qwen2.5-coder) runs multi-turn tool loops far faster than a large multimodal model, which reprocesses the whole prompt every turn.

data.name: LabelModelRolesAdvanced
data.value: Use a separate model per role (advanced)

data.name: HintModelRolesAdvanced
data.value: By default the chat model is used everywhere. Enable this to assign a different model to the agent, code actions, inline completion (FIM) and inline edit.

data.name: HintAgentMaxIterations
data.value: Maximum number of Plan → Act → Observe cycles the agent may run per request. 0 falls back to the default (20). Default: 20.

data.name: HintTaskTimeoutQuick
data.value: Deadline for short tasks: explain, fix, doc, inline edit, agent plan. Default: 120 s.

data.name: HintTaskTimeoutNormal
data.value: Deadline per agent turn for tool-enabled chat and orchestrator steps. Default: 300 s.

data.name: HintTaskTimeoutDeep
data.value: Reserved for extended reasoning tasks. Default: 600 s.

data.name: AgentPlanPrompt
data.value: Before using any tools, output ONLY a JSON object with the following structure (no prose, no markdown fences): {"goal":"one-sentence description of what you will do","steps":[{"i":1,"desc":"what this step does","tool":"expected_tool_name_or_null"},{"i":2,"desc":"..."}]} Include 2–6 concrete steps. Use null for "tool" if no specific tool is expected. After outputting the JSON, stop — do not call any tools yet.

data.name: AgentExecutePlan
data.value: Good. Now execute the plan step by step, starting from step 1. Do NOT write any explanatory text — immediately call the appropriate tool for step 1 right now.

data.name: AgentPlanFallbackStep
data.value: Execute the request using available tools

data.name: AgentObservePrompt
data.value: [OBSERVE — {0}/{1}] Tools executed: {2}. Remaining plan steps: {3}. Immediately call the tool for the next step — no preamble, no explanation. If the goal is fully achieved, write the final answer to my most recent request without calling any more tools — do not repeat an earlier answer from this conversation.

data.name: AgentObservePromptComplete
data.value: [OBSERVE — {0}/{1}] Tools executed: {2}. All plan steps are complete. Now write the complete final answer to my most recent request, which was: "{3}" — directly, in full, and in my language, WITHOUT calling any more tools. Only call another tool if essential information is still missing. Do not repeat an answer given to an earlier, different question.

data.name: AgentNudgeToolCall
data.value: You described your intentions but did not call any tools. Do NOT produce any text — call the first tool from your plan RIGHT NOW.

data.name: AgentSynthesizePrompt
data.value: You have gathered enough information with the tools above. Do NOT call any more tools. Using those results, write the complete final answer to my most recent request, which was: "{0}" — directly, in full, and in my language. Answer THAT request specifically; do not repeat an earlier answer from this conversation.

data.name: MsgFirstRunWelcome
data.value:
✨ **First launch** — {0} available. Auto-selected **{1}** as default model.
Open **Settings** to change the model or configure a dedicated model for code actions and inline completions.

data.name: MsgFirstRunNoModels
data.value:
⚠ **First launch** — Ollama is running but no chat models were found.
Pull a model to get started: ``` ollama pull llama3.1 ``` Then restart Visual Studio (or open Settings and click **Test**).

data.name: MsgFirstRunBackendDown
data.value:
⚠ **First launch** — the model server is not reachable at **{0}**.
Start Ollama and click **↻ Retry** in the header: ``` ollama serve ```

data.name: MsgFirstRunVramWarning
data.value: ⚠ **VRAM** — the selected models need about **{0} GB** together, more than your **{1} GB** budget. They may spill to system RAM and run slowly. Consider a smaller model, or run `/hardware` to review.

data.name: HintMcpEnabled
data.value: When enabled, the configured Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers are launched on startup and their tools become available to the agent. The server commands (e.g. npx, uvx) must be installed on this machine.

data.name: HintMcpServers
data.value: MCP server definitions as a JSON object keyed by server name (Claude Desktop / Continue format), each with command, args and env. Stdio transport only. Discovered tools are exposed to the agent prefixed with mcp__.

data.name: McpCancelled
data.value: MCP tool call cancelled by user.

data.name: PermissionBlockedHard
data.value: Blocked by the built-in safety denylist (catastrophic command): {0}

data.name: LabelMcpHttpServer
data.value: HTTP server (use a URL instead of a command)

data.name: McpValidationDuplicate
data.value: A server with this name already exists.

data.name: HintMcpEditServer
data.value: Edit this MCP server's settings (name, command, arguments, environment variables).

data.name: HintMcpDeleteServer
data.value: Remove this MCP server from the list.

data.name: HintRowDelete
data.value: Remove this entry from the list.

data.name: LabelSlashText
data.value: Prompt text — use {args} for extra words

data.name: SlashValidationNameText
data.value: A name starting with / and prompt text are required.

data.name: SlashValidationDuplicate
data.value: A command with this name already exists.

data.name: ToolValidationNameCommand
data.value: A tool name and command are required.

data.name: ToolValidationDuplicate
data.value: A tool with this name already exists.

data.name: DocsNoSites
data.value: No documentation indexed yet. Add a source with `/docs add <url> [title]`.

data.name: DocsAdded
data.value: 📚 Added **{0}** — crawling and indexing in the background…

data.name: DocsNotReady
data.value: No documentation is indexed yet. Add one with `/docs add <url>`. ({0})

data.name: SlashHintFetch
data.value: show a web page as text

data.name: SlashHintGit
data.value: git status, log and diff summary

data.name: SlashHintMap
data.value: show project architecture map (namespaces, types, hotspots)

data.name: SlashHintFixBuild
data.value: compile → AI fixes → recompile until clean

data.name: SlashHintIndex
data.value: show or rebuild the RAG codebase index

data.name: SlashHintTemplate
data.value: start a session from a preset template — /template <id>

data.name: SlashHintDiff
data.value: attach full git diff as context chip — /diff [path]

data.name: SlashHintCheck
data.value: AI-review the current git diff against .inferpal/checks — /check [name|init]

data.name: SlashHintSnippets
data.value: list saved code snippets — /snippets [copy <n> | delete <n> | clear]

data.name: SlashHintAgentStep
data.value: toggle agent step mode — pause after each tool call

data.name: SlashHintResume
data.value: resume agent after a step-mode pause

data.name: SlashHintPlan
data.value: toggle plan mode — read-only: explore and propose a plan, no edits

data.name: SlashHintHardware
data.value: show GPU/VRAM profile — /hardware [gb] to set the VRAM budget

data.name: SlashHintSetup
data.value: re-run first-run setup — re-detect backend, re-pick models

data.name: HardwareNoVramBackend
data.value: ℹ️ Live VRAM and per-model monitoring isn't available with this backend. The manual VRAM budget (`/hardware <gb>`) still drives fit-checks.

data.name: ModelsBackendUnsupported
data.value: ℹ️ Model management (pull, delete, running) is only available with the Ollama backend.

data.name: SnippetsNone
data.value: No snippets saved yet. Use the ⭐ button on any code block to save one.

data.name: NoteUsage
data.value: Usage: `/note <text>` — appends a timestamped note to `.inferpal/notes.md`.

data.name: NotesNoneYet
data.value: No project notes yet. Use `/note <text>` to add one.

data.name: ModelsNoneRunning
data.value: No models currently loaded in VRAM.

data.name: ModelsNoneInstalled
data.value: No models installed. Use `/models pull <name>` to download one.

data.name: SlashHintUndoRun
data.value: Undo all file changes from the last agent run

data.name: UndoRunNone
data.value: No agent run with file changes to undo in this session.

data.name: UndoRunResult
data.value: ↩ Last run undone — {0} file(s) restored, {1} deleted.

data.name: HardwareInstalledModelsTable
data.value:
### Installed models
| Model | Disk | Est. VRAM | |---|---|---|

data.name: HardwareInstalledNote
data.value: _Installed-model VRAM figures are estimates (disk size × 1.2, covering KV-cache + runtime)._

data.name: HardwareCtxWarn
data.value: ⚠ Your num_ctx ({0}) exceeds what fits the VRAM budget — the KV-cache may spill to system RAM and slow generation. Consider lowering it to {1}.

data.name: HintVramBudget
data.value: Total VRAM (in GB) available on the Ollama host. Used to recommend a safe num_ctx and to warn when the selected models won't fit. Leave empty to auto-detect on a local host; a manual value is required for remote hosts. Also settable with `/hardware <gb>`.

data.name: TooltipAgentMode
data.value: Switch between Chat and Agent mode (autonomous Plan → Act → Observe). Wired to the 'Enable autonomous agent mode' setting.
